title: Strip .takt/beads/ paths from worker-reported touched_files / changed_files at finalize time
description:
Bug: developer beads in this repo end up with `touched_files` and `changed_files` lists containing 1000+ `.takt/beads/B-XXX.json` paths that the bead never actually touched. The bloat propagates aggressively through the followup chain (tester/docs/review beads inherit and union it via `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files`, `_create_followups`, and `_sync_followup_scope`), permanently inflating every subsequent bead's JSON.

Token cost: ~30 chars per entry × 1000+ entries = ~40 KB per affected bead. Across a feature's followup chain plus correctives, a single feature can carry several hundred KB of redundant filenames. Every `takt bead show <id>` and every worker reading dependency handoffs pays this cost in context window.

## Root cause

Workers (Claude/Codex agents) populate `touched_files` and `changed_files` themselves in their structured handoff (per the schema in `src/agent_takt/runner.py:145-186`). The natural way to compute these fields is `git diff main..HEAD --name-only` from the feature worktree.

In takt feature worktrees, the FIRST commit on the branch is `chore: untrack bead state from feature branch [skip ci]` — generated by `WorktreeManager._protect_worktree_bead_state()` running `git rm --cached` on every tracked `.takt/beads/*.json` file at worktree creation. In this repo (and any takt project that opts to track `.takt/beads/` in git), that's 1000+ rm-cached deletions per chore commit.

When the worker computes `git diff main..HEAD --name-only`, those deletions appear as "files this branch touched" — and the worker dutifully includes them in its handoff. takt then persists the bloated lists to the bead JSON.

## Required fix

Filter `.takt/beads/**` out of `touched_files` and `changed_files` at the point where takt converts the worker's reported lists into the bead's stored fields. Single point of intervention, prevents the bloat from ever being persisted, automatically un-bloats downstream propagation (followups, sync, aggregation) because they have nothing to propagate.

In `src/agent_takt/scheduler/finalize.py`:

1. Add a private module-level helper near the top of the file:

   ```python
   _BEAD_STATE_NOISE_PREFIX = ".takt/beads/"

   def _strip_bead_state_paths(paths: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:
       """Remove .takt/beads/ paths from worker-reported file lists.

       Workers commonly compute touched_files via `git diff main..HEAD
       --name-only`, which surfaces the safety-net's chore-commit deletions
       of bead state files. Those entries are pure noise — bead state is
       takt's operational metadata, not work product — and inflate every
       bead JSON and downstream followup.
       """
       return [p for p in paths if not p.startswith(_BEAD_STATE_NOISE_PREFIX)]
   ```

   (Use `from typing import Iterable` if not already imported.)

2. Apply the filter at the two existing assignment sites where worker results are copied into the bead's stored fields:

   At line 66 (currently `bead.touched_files = list(agent_result.touched_files)`):
   ```python
   bead.touched_files = _strip_bead_state_paths(agent_result.touched_files)
   ```

   At line 101 (currently `bead.changed_files = list(agent_result.changed_files)`):
   ```python
   bead.changed_files = _strip_bead_state_paths(agent_result.changed_files)
   ```

   Also apply at line 326 / 327 (the recovery-bead handoff application path) so recovery beads get the same treatment:
   ```python
   original.touched_files = _strip_bead_state_paths(agent_result.touched_files or original.touched_files)
   original.changed_files = _strip_bead_state_paths(agent_result.changed_files or original.changed_files)
   ```

3. The handoff_summary fields written via `storage.update_bead(...)` calls (`finalize.py:94`, `finalize.py:350`) should ALSO use the stripped versions. Look for any other site that constructs a `HandoffSummary` from `agent_result.touched_files` / `agent_result.changed_files` and apply the filter consistently.

## Test (regression)

In `tests/test_scheduler_finalize.py` (or wherever existing finalize tests live; if no existing test file fits, add to whichever scheduler test file already covers `BeadExecutor`/finalization). Add ONE focused test:

```python
def test_finalize_strips_bead_state_paths_from_worker_lists(self) -> None:
    """Worker-reported `.takt/beads/` paths must be filtered from
    touched_files and changed_files before being persisted on the bead.

    Workers compute these fields from `git diff main..HEAD --name-only`,
    which includes the safety-net chore commit's rm-cached deletions of
    every bead state file. Persisting that noise inflates every bead by
    tens of KB and propagates through followups.
    """
    # Simulate a worker handoff with both legitimate code changes and
    # spurious bead state files.
    bead = self.storage.create_bead(
        title="Test bead", agent_type="developer", description="..."
    )
    agent_result = AgentRunResult(
        ...,
        touched_files=[
            "src/foo.py",
            "src/bar.py",
            ".takt/beads/B-aaa.json",
            ".takt/beads/B-bbb.json",
            ".takt/beads/.gitkeep",
        ],
        changed_files=[
            "src/foo.py",
            ".takt/beads/B-aaa.json",
        ],
        ...
    )
    # Run finalize (or whatever method copies agent_result onto the bead)
    finalize_bead(bead, agent_result, ...)

    # The persisted lists must contain only the legitimate code paths.
    self.assertEqual(["src/foo.py", "src/bar.py"], bead.touched_files)
    self.assertEqual(["src/foo.py"], bead.changed_files)
```

Adjust the exact fixture pattern to match existing finalize tests (use `FakeRunner` / `OrchestratorTests` helpers from `tests/helpers.py` if applicable).

## What this fix does NOT change

1. Does NOT modify `WorktreeManager._protect_worktree_bead_state` or the chore commit it generates. Removing the chore commit would solve this differently but would also weaken the safety net; the filter is the targeted fix.
2. Does NOT modify the worker handoff schema (`runner.py:145-186`). Workers still report whatever they observed; takt just sanitizes the input.
3. Does NOT touch `_create_followups`, `_sync_followup_scope`, or `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files` in `scheduler/followups.py`. Once finalize is clean, propagation has nothing to propagate. The fix is upstream of all aggregation.
4. Does NOT filter other `.takt/**` subdirs (`.takt/logs/`, `.takt/telemetry/`, `.takt/agent-runs/`, `.takt/worktrees/`, `.takt/config.yaml`, `.takt/version.json`). Only `.takt/beads/**` is the documented noise source. If other patterns surface in real bead JSONs after this fix, expand the filter in a follow-up bead.
5. Does NOT retroactively clean up existing bloated bead JSONs on disk. Going forward, new beads will be clean. A separate housekeeping bead can prune historical bloat if the operator wants.

## Acceptance criteria

1. After this fix, a finalized bead's `touched_files` and `changed_files` arrays contain ZERO entries starting with `.takt/beads/`, regardless of what the worker handoff reported.
2. The new regression test passes; it explicitly seeds a worker handoff with mixed legitimate paths and `.takt/beads/` noise and asserts the noise is dropped while legitimate paths survive.
3. Existing finalize-related tests continue to pass (no behavior change for non-`.takt/beads/` paths).
4. `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` passes overall with no regressions.
5. Followup propagation (`_create_followups`, `_sync_followup_scope`, `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files`) automatically benefits — no code change needed there. A subjective check: spawn a small feature with a developer bead, look at the resulting tester/docs/review beads' touched_files. They should NOT contain hundreds of bead state paths.

## References

- Bug discussion in operator session (2026-05-06)
- `src/agent_takt/scheduler/finalize.py:60-101` — where worker results become bead state (primary edit site)
- `src/agent_takt/scheduler/finalize.py:325-345` — recovery bead handoff application (secondary edit site)
- `src/agent_takt/scheduler/followups.py:381-402` — `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files` aggregation (no edits, but verify it benefits)
- `src/agent_takt/runner.py:145-186` — worker handoff schema (read-only context)
- CLAUDE.md "Shared followup scope population" subsection — describes the aggregation that compounds the bloat

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
